darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare and valuable.

name: Metal Repeating Heavy Crossbow
description: Holds 5 bolts. Reloading lever is a free action. Loading a new case is a full-round action.
darkSunNotes: A true metal weapon — an object of incredible value on Athas. It will not shatter against bone or obsidian weapons. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Bone Repeating Light Crossbow
breakageNote: On a natural 1 attack roll, make a DC 12 Reflex save or the weapon breaks.
description: Holds 5 bolts. Reloading lever is a free action.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare and valuable.

name: Metal Repeating Light Crossbow
description: Holds 5 bolts. Reloading lever is a free action.
darkSunNotes: A true metal weapon — an object of incredible value on Athas. It will not shatter against bone or obsidian weapons. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Net
description: A fighting net with small barbs and a trailing rope. Make a ranged touch attack to entangle enemies.

name: Bone Shuriken (5)
breakageNote: On a natural 1 attack roll, make a DC 12 Reflex save or the weapon breaks.
description: A special monk weapon. Treated as ammunition for drawing purposes.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare and valuable.

name: Metal Shuriken (5)
description: A special monk weapon. Treated as ammunition for drawing purposes.
darkSunNotes: A true metal weapon — an object of incredible value on Athas. It will not shatter against bone or obsidian weapons. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Padded Armor
description: Quilted layers of cloth and batting.

name: Leather Armor
description: The breastplate and shoulder protectors are made of leather stiffened by boiling in oil.

name: Hide Studded Leather
description: Tough but flexible leather reinforced with close-set rivets. On Athas, bone studs are used instead of metal.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from hide. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Studded Leather
description: Tough but flexible leather reinforced with close-set rivets. On Athas, bone studs are used instead of metal.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Chitin Chain Shirt
description: Protects the torso while leaving limbs free. Includes a layer of quilted fabric underneath.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from chitin. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Chain Shirt
description: Protects the torso while leaving limbs free. Includes a layer of quilted fabric underneath.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Hide Armor
description: Prepared from multiple layers of leather and animal hides. Stiff and hard to move in. Favored by druids.

name: Chitin Scale Mail
description: A coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of hard material, much like scales.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from chitin. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Scale Mail
description: A coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of hard material, much like scales.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Chitin Chainmail
description: Interlocking rings covering the body. Includes quilted fabric underneath and gauntlets.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from chitin. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Chainmail
description: Interlocking rings covering the body. Includes quilted fabric underneath and gauntlets.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Bone Splint Mail
description: Narrow vertical strips riveted to leather backing, worn over cloth padding. Flexible chainmail protects joints.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Splint Mail
description: Narrow vertical strips riveted to leather backing, worn over cloth padding. Flexible chainmail protects joints.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Bone Banded Mail
description: Overlapping strips sewn to leather and chainmail backing. Strips cover vulnerable areas, chain and leather protect joints.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Banded Mail
description: Overlapping strips sewn to leather and chainmail backing. Strips cover vulnerable areas, chain and leather protect joints.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Bone Full Plate
description: Shaped and fitted plates riveted and interlocked to cover the entire body. Must be individually fitted by a master armorsmith.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from bone. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Full Plate
description: Shaped and fitted plates riveted and interlocked to cover the entire body. Must be individually fitted by a master armorsmith.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Wood Buckler
description: A small shield worn strapped to the forearm. You can use a bow or crossbow without penalty. You can't bash with it.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from wood. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Buckler
description: A small shield worn strapped to the forearm. You can use a bow or crossbow without penalty. You can't bash with it.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Wood Heavy Shield
description: Strapped to the forearm and gripped with the hand. Too heavy to use that hand for anything else.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from wood. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Heavy Shield
description: Strapped to the forearm and gripped with the hand. Too heavy to use that hand for anything else.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Tower Shield
description: Massive shield nearly as tall as the wielder. Can provide total cover at the cost of attacks. -2 penalty on attack rolls.

name: Armor Spikes
description: Spikes on your armor for extra piercing damage on grapple attacks. Count as a martial weapon.
darkSunNotes: Requires metal. Extraordinarily rare and expensive on Athas.

name: Locked Gauntlet
description: Chains and braces that attach a weapon so it can't be dropped. +10 bonus against being disarmed.
darkSunNotes: Requires metal. Extraordinarily rare and expensive on Athas.

name: Wood Shield Spikes
description: Turn a shield into a martial piercing weapon that increases bash damage.
darkSunNotes: The common Athasian version, made from wood. Metal versions are extraordinarily rare.

name: Metal Shield Spikes
description: Turn a shield into a martial piercing weapon that increases bash damage.
darkSunNotes: A true metal armor — an object of incredible value on Athas. Possessing metal marks you as extremely wealthy or dangerous.

name: Backpack
description: A leather pack carried on the back, typically with straps to secure it.

name: Bedroll
description: Bedding and a blanket thin enough to be rolled up and tied. In an emergency, it can double as a stretcher.

name: Winter Blanket
description: A thick, quilted, wool blanket made to keep you warm in cold weather.

name: Caltrops
description: Four-pronged iron spikes. One 2-pound bag covers a 5-foot square. Stepping on one deals 1 point of damage and reduces speed by half. On Athas, often made from bone shards.
darkSunNotes: Athasian caltrops are typically made from sharpened bone or chitin rather than iron.

name: Candle
description: Dimly illuminates a 5-foot radius and burns for 1 hour.

name: Chain (10 ft.)
description: Hardness 10, 5 hit points. Can be burst with a DC 26 Strength check.
darkSunNotes: Metal chains are a sign of great wealth on Athas. Rope is far more common.

name: Crowbar
description: An iron bar for levering open doors, chests, and chains. Grants a +2 circumstance bonus on Strength checks for prying.
darkSunNotes: A metal crowbar is valuable on Athas. Bone substitutes exist but are less effective.

name: Flint and Steel
description: Striking steel and flint creates sparks. Lighting a torch is a full-round action.

name: Grappling Hook
description: Secures rope to protrusions. Throwing requires a Use Rope check (DC 10, +2 per 10 feet).
darkSunNotes: Metal grappling hooks are uncommon. Bone hooks are cheaper but less reliable.

name: Hammer
description: A one-handed hammer with an iron head, useful for pounding pitons into a wall.

name: Ink (1 oz. vial)
description: Black ink. Other colors cost twice as much.

name: Bullseye Lantern
description: Clear illumination in a 60-foot cone, shadowy in 120-foot cone. Burns 6 hours on a pint of oil.
darkSunNotes: Metal lanterns are luxury items on Athas.

name: Hooded Lantern
description: Shuttered sides. Clearly illuminates 30-foot radius, shadowy in 60-foot radius. Burns 6 hours on a pint of oil.
darkSunNotes: Metal lanterns are luxury items on Athas.

name: Manacles
description: Binds a Medium creature. Escape Artist DC 30. Strength DC 26 to break. Hardness 10, 10 hp.
darkSunNotes: Metal manacles are used primarily by templars and wealthy slavers on Athas.

name: Masterwork Manacles
description: Escape Artist DC 35. Strength DC 28 to break. Hardness 10, 10 hp.
darkSunNotes: A prized possession among templars.

name: Small Steel Mirror
description: A polished steel mirror for looking around corners, signaling, or checking for medusas.
darkSunNotes: Extremely rare on Athas. Polished obsidian mirrors are more common but less effective.

name: Oil (1-pint flask)
description: Burns 6 hours in a lantern. Can be used as a splash weapon. Covers a 5-foot square; if lit, burns 2 rounds dealing 1d3 fire damage.

name: Piton
description: A steel spike with an eye for looping rope, used for climbing.
darkSunNotes: Metal pitons are uncommon. Bone pitons exist but are less durable.

name: Pole, 10-foot
description: When you suspect a trap, you can use this instead of reaching in with your hand.

name: Portable Ram
description: +2 circumstance bonus on Strength checks to break open doors. Allows a second person to help without rolling.

name: Trail Rations (per day)
description: Compact, dry, high-energy foods: jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts.

name: Hempen Rope (50 ft.)
description: 2 hit points, burst DC 23 Strength check.

name: Silk Rope (50 ft.)
description: 4 hit points, burst DC 24. Provides +2 on Use Rope checks.
darkSunNotes: Silk is rare on Athas. Giant hair rope is a more common alternative.

name: Spyglass
description: Magnifies objects to twice their size.
darkSunNotes: An object of extraordinary rarity and value on Athas.

name: Tent
description: A simple tent that sleeps two.

name: Torch
description: Burns 1 hour, clearly illuminating 20-foot radius, shadowy out to 40 feet.

name: Waterskin
description: A leather pouch for holding water.
darkSunNotes: Essential survival gear on Athas. Water is life in the desert.

name: Acid (flask)
description: Splash weapon (ranged touch, 10 ft.). Direct hit: 1d6 acid. Splash: 1 point acid.

name: Alchemist's Fire (flask)
description: Sticky, adhesive substance that ignites in air. Direct hit: 1d6 fire + 1d6 next round. Splash: 1 point fire. DC 15 Reflex to extinguish.

name: Antitoxin (vial)
description: +5 alchemical bonus on Fortitude saves against poison for 1 hour.

name: Everburning Torch
description: A torch with a continual flame spell. Clearly illuminates 20-foot radius, shadowy out to 40 feet.

name: Holy Water (flask)
description: Damages undead and evil outsiders. Direct hit: 2d4 damage. Splash: 1 point damage.

name: Smokestick
description: Creates thick smoke filling a 10-foot cube (as fog cloud). Consumed after 1 round.

name: Sunrod
description: Gold-tipped iron rod that glows when struck. Illuminates 30-foot radius clearly, 60-foot shadowy. Lasts 6 hours.

name: Tanglefoot Bag
description: Ranged touch (10 ft.). Entangles target. DC 15 Reflex or glued to floor. DC 17 Strength or 15 damage to break free.

name: Thunderstone
description: Creates deafening bang. 10-foot radius, DC 15 Fortitude or deafened 1 hour.

name: Tindertwig
description: Ignites when struck against a rough surface. Lighting a torch is a standard action.

name: Alchemist's Lab
description: Beakers, bottles, chemicals. +2 circumstance bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks.

name: Artisan's Tools
description: Tools needed to pursue any craft. Without them, -2 on Craft checks.

name: Masterwork Artisan's Tools
description: +2 circumstance bonus on Craft checks.

name: Climber's Kit
description: Special pitons, boot tips, gloves, and harness. +2 circumstance bonus on Climb checks.

name: Disguise Kit
description: Cosmetics, hair dye, physical props. +2 on Disguise checks. Exhausted after ten uses.

name: Healer's Kit
description: Herbs, salves, bandages. +2 on Heal checks. Exhausted after ten uses.

name: Holy Symbol, Wooden
description: Focuses positive energy for cleric/paladin spells and turning undead.

name: Holy Symbol, Silver
description: A silver holy symbol. Works no better than wooden but serves as a mark of status.
darkSunNotes: Silver is almost as rare as steel on Athas.

name: Magnifying Glass
description: +2 on Appraise checks for small/detailed items. Can start fires with sunlight.
darkSunNotes: Glass objects are extremely rare on Athas.

name: Spell Component Pouch
description: Contains all material components and focuses for spellcasting except those with a specific cost.

name: Spellbook, Wizard's (Blank)
description: 100 pages of parchment, each spell takes one page per spell level.

name: Thieves' Tools
description: Skeleton keys, picks, pries, clamp, hand saw. Required for Disable Device and Open Lock.

name: Masterwork Thieves' Tools
description: +2 circumstance bonus on Disable Device and Open Lock checks.

name: Arrows (20)
description: A quiver of 20 arrows for use with any bow. On Athas, arrows typically have bone or obsidian tips.
darkSunNotes: Bone-tipped arrows are standard. Metal-tipped arrows are rare and deal +1 damage.

name: Crossbow Bolts (10)
description: A case of 10 crossbow bolts.

name: Sling Bullets (10)
description: Lead sling bullets. On Athas, stone bullets are used instead.
darkSunNotes: Athasian sling bullets are typically polished stones.

name: The Belt of Kings
description: The Belt of Kings is an ancient artifact crafted during the Green Age, a wide belt made of interlocking plates of an unknown silvery metal. It was originally created to aid rulers in governing their people wisely. The belt grants the wearer powerful telepathic abilities, including the power to read the thoughts and emotions of all creatures within a 100-foot radius. The wearer gains a +6 enhancement bonus to Charisma, can cast suggestion at will, and can use mass suggestion 3/day. Additionally, the Belt of Kings grants the wearer immunity to all charm and compulsion effects, and the ability to detect lies automatically.
darkSunNotes: This artifact dates back to the Green Age of Athas, before the world was blighted by defiling magic. Its current whereabouts are unknown, though legends suggest it lies hidden in the ruins beneath the city of Giustenal.

grantedPowers.name: Read Thoughts
grantedPowers.description: Read thoughts and emotions of all creatures within 100 ft.

grantedPowers.name: Suggestion
grantedPowers.description: As the spell, at will (DC 25)

grantedPowers.name: Mass Suggestion
grantedPowers.description: As the spell, 3/day

name: The Dark Lens
description: The Dark Lens is perhaps the most powerful and dangerous artifact on all of Athas. It is a massive obsidian orb, roughly two feet in diameter, that pulses with an inner dark radiance. Created by Rajaat the War-Bringer himself, the Dark Lens was the key component in the creation of the Champions of Rajaat. The lens can focus and amplify psionic and magical energy to incredible levels. It can channel spell energy to power epic transformations, amplify any psionic power to double its normal effect, create a conduit to the Black, and serve as a focus for mass destruction spells affecting entire regions. Any creature touching it without proper attunement takes 10d6 damage per round.
darkSunNotes: The Dark Lens was hidden by the Champions after they imprisoned Rajaat in the Hollow beneath the Black. It was later recovered by Tithian of Tyr. Currently believed to be lost in the Sea of Silt.

grantedPowers.name: Amplify Power
grantedPowers.description: Double the range and area of any psionic power manifested through the lens

grantedPowers.name: Disintegrate
grantedPowers.description: As the power, 1/day

grantedPowers.name: Defiling Amplification
grantedPowers.description: Channel defiling energy through the lens, devastating all plant life within 1 mile

name: Heart of the Drake
description: A crystalline artifact shaped like a large, faceted gemstone that pulses with crimson light—the preserved and psionically enhanced heart of an ancient drake. Grants the ability to manifest any psychokinesis power up to 6th level at will, fire resistance 30, and drake form transformation 1/day for up to 1 hour. In drake form: claws (2d6), bite (2d8), fly speed 60 ft., breath weapon (10d6 fire, 60-foot cone, Reflex DC 25 half). Each transformation drains 2 Constitution permanently.
darkSunNotes: Drakes are nearly extinct on Athas. This heart was taken from one of the last great drakes during the Cleansing Wars. Last known location: the treasury of a powerful psionicist of the Order.

grantedPowers.name: Fire Ray
grantedPowers.description: Ranged touch attack, 1d6 fire damage (energy ray, fire)

grantedPowers.name: Fireball
grantedPowers.description: 7d6 fire damage in 20-ft. radius (energy ball, fire)

grantedPowers.name: Fire Immunity
grantedPowers.description: Complete immunity to fire damage while active

name: The Heartwood Spear
description: A legendary +5 spear fashioned from heartwood of a Green Age tree. Deals additional 2d6 damage to defilers and undead. The wielder can cast plant growth at will, and land within 1 mile becomes lush within 24 hours. Control plants and wall of thorns 3/day each. No creature can be raised as undead in its presence, and the wielder is immune to energy drain.
darkSunNotes: Most sought-after artifact among the Veiled Alliance and druids. Legend holds it was created by the last great druid of the Green Age. Its wielder is prophesied to restore Athas.

grantedPowers.name: Anti-Defiling Ward
grantedPowers.description: Negate defiling within 30 ft. radius

grantedPowers.name: Smite Undead
grantedPowers.description: +4d6 damage vs. undead on a successful hit

grantedPowers.name: Preserve Life
grantedPowers.description: Heal all allies within 30 ft. for 3d8+5 HP

name: Ktandeo's Cane
description: A gnarled wooden staff topped with a clear crystal, owned by preserver Ktandeo of the Veiled Alliance in Tyr. Functions as a staff of power with preserving enchantments. Stores up to 50 spell levels of preserver spells. Grants +4 to Intelligence and Wisdom, SR 25, and allows casting preserver spells of 5th level or lower without material components. Can convert defiling magic to preserving magic within 30 feet.
darkSunNotes: Ktandeo mentored Sadira of Tyr. His cane was instrumental in liberating Tyr from King Kalak.

grantedPowers.name: Store Spell
grantedPowers.description: Store one preserver spell of 5th level or lower

grantedPowers.name: Anti-Defiling Shield
grantedPowers.description: Prevent defiling within 20 ft.

grantedPowers.name: Release Spell
grantedPowers.description: Release a stored spell as a standard action

name: The Last Tree
description: A sentient, psionically aware tree over 200 feet tall with a 30-foot trunk. Int 20, Wis 25, Cha 18. Communicates telepathically within 1 mile. Manifests any telepathy or clairsentience power up to 9th level at will. Within its 5-mile radius: defiling magic is impossible, healing is maximized, land is permanently fertile. Attuned druids gain 9th-level divine spells. Can create Guardian Seedlings to restore land elsewhere.
darkSunNotes: Hidden in the Forest Ridge, protected by halfling tribes and druidic wards. The Sorcerer-Kings have sought it for millennia.

grantedPowers.name: Telepathic Bond
grantedPowers.description: Communicate telepathically with any creature within 1 mile

grantedPowers.name: Life Nexus
grantedPowers.description: Channel life energy to heal all allies within 100 ft. for 5d8 HP

grantedPowers.name: Anti-Defiling Aura
grantedPowers.description: Nullify all defiling magic within 100 ft.

name: Orbs of Kalid-Ma
description: Seven crystalline spheres (4 inches each) containing fragments of Sorcerer-King Kalid-Ma's consciousness. Each orb grants telepathy 100 ft., 4th-level telepathy powers 3/day, and +2 Intelligence. Multiple orbs compound: 2 orbs = 5th-level powers, etc. All seven united grant: any psionic power at will, immunity to mind-affecting effects, dominate any creature within 1 mile (Will DC 35). Risks awakening Kalid-Ma's consciousness.
darkSunNotes: Kalid-Ma was destroyed in Kalidnay. The orbs are scattered across the Tablelands. At least one is held by the Shadow King of Nibenay.

grantedPowers.name: Dominate
grantedPowers.description: Dominate a single creature (Will DC 25)

grantedPowers.name: Mass Domination
grantedPowers.description: Dominate multiple creatures within 30 ft.

grantedPowers.name: Sentient Will
grantedPowers.description: The Orbs resist being set down — Will DC 30 to release voluntarily

name: The Planar Gate
description: A 20-foot-tall, 15-foot-wide arch of unknown gray metal. Creates stable portals to other planes—normally nearly impossible on Athas due to the Gray. Requires 200+ power points. Remains open 1 hour. Accesses Astral, Elemental Planes, and the Gray. Each activation weakens the planar barrier for 1d4 weeks.
darkSunNotes: Believed to be in the ruins of Giustenal. The Order seeks to control it for access to extraplanar resources.

grantedPowers.name: Plane Shift
grantedPowers.description: Open a portal to another plane of existence

grantedPowers.name: Stable Portal
grantedPowers.description: Create a stable two-way portal lasting 1 hour

name: The Psionatrix
description: A small, perfect crystal orb that dampens or nullifies all psionic activity across Athas. Wielder chooses range from 1 mile to worldwide. Affected area: all psionic powers fail, focus lost, items cease functioning. Wielder is immune and gains: +5 caster level, halved power point costs, +5 save DCs. Absorbs up to 500 power points/day from suppressed psionicists.
darkSunNotes: Created by the Order as a weapon of last resort. Activated once during the Cleansing Wars with devastating consequences. Its location is the Order's most closely guarded secret.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Dampening
grantedPowers.description: Suppress all psionic activity within 1 mile for 1 hour

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Amplification
grantedPowers.description: All psionic powers manifested by the wielder cost 2 fewer PSP (min 1)

name: The Scorcher
description: A +5 flaming burst keen longsword forged by Hamanu of Urik. Deals additional 3d6 fire on every hit. 60-foot fire cone 3/day (15d6, Reflex DC 28 half). Grants fire immunity, see through fire/smoke, and teleport between open flames within 1 mile. Intelligent (Int 17, Wis 14, Cha 18, Ego 25) with its own agenda to burn the world clean.
darkSunNotes: Wielded by Hamanu during the Cleansing Wars. Said to have destroyed entire cities. Kept in Hamanu's vault in Urik.

grantedPowers.name: Scorching Ray
grantedPowers.description: 3d6 fire damage ranged touch, at will

grantedPowers.name: Teleport
grantedPowers.description: Teleport up to 1 mile, 3/day

grantedPowers.name: Flaming Blade
grantedPowers.description: +2d6 fire damage on melee attacks

name: The Scourge of Rkard
description: A +5 multi-tailed whip of unknown flexible metal with 20-foot reach. On hit: Will DC 30 or one random psionic power suppressed 1d4 rounds. Critical hits drain 2d10 power points to wielder. Psychic scream 3/day: 30-foot radius, 9d6 psychic damage, Will DC 28 half, dazed 1d4 rounds on failed save. Created by Rkard, last dwarven king of Kemalok.
darkSunNotes: Created by Rkard to fight the Sorcerer-Kings' armies. Lost when Kemalok fell. Its recovery is the focus of many dwarven adventurers.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Drain
grantedPowers.description: On a successful hit, drain 1d6 PSP from the target and gain them

grantedPowers.name: Extended Reach
grantedPowers.description: Extend the scourge to 15 ft. reach

grantedPowers.name: Anti-Psionic Strike
grantedPowers.description: On hit, target must save or lose access to psionic powers for 1d4 rounds

name: The Centennial Brain
description: A large, pulsating organic mass (3 feet diameter) containing 1,000 years of halfling knowledge from the Blue Age. Answers questions with 90% accuracy. Manifests clairsentience/telepathy powers up to 7th level 3/day. Can teach new psionic powers in 1d4 weeks at half XP cost. Must be fed 1 pound of fresh organic matter daily.
darkSunNotes: Located somewhere in the Forest Ridge, tended by secretive halfling druids. Contains lost life-shaping knowledge.

grantedPowers.name: Knowledge Repository
grantedPowers.description: +10 to any Knowledge check, at will

grantedPowers.name: Telepathic Library
grantedPowers.description: Access stored memories spanning thousands of years

grantedPowers.name: Mindlink
grantedPowers.description: Establish telepathic link with willing creature

name: The Corundrum Wormskin
description: Living armor from a Blue Age burrowing creature. Functions as +5 full plate with no max Dex, no armor check penalty, no spell failure. Grants DR 10/-, regeneration 5, acid immunity. Can disguise as clothing. Burrow 30 ft. 3/day. Self-heals 5 hp/hour. Detaches and seeks new host if wearer dies.
darkSunNotes: One of the most remarkable life-shaped artifacts. Designed for Blue Age life-shaper warriors. Only a handful survive.

grantedPowers.name: Living Armor
grantedPowers.description: +8 natural armor bonus, no max Dex penalty

grantedPowers.name: Regeneration
grantedPowers.description: Fast healing 3 while worn

grantedPowers.name: Burrow
grantedPowers.description: Burrow speed 20 ft. for 10 minutes

name: The Green Rhul
description: An acorn-sized seed glowing with soft green light—the last seed of the original Life Tree. When planted, grows into a full Life Tree in 24 hours: a massive organic laboratory for life-shaping. Generates 10 lbs of raw organic material daily. Can be harvested (destroying the tree) and replanted. Only one Life Tree can exist at a time.
darkSunNotes: The most sacred artifact among Forest Ridge halflings. Wars have been fought over it. Outsiders who learn of it rarely leave the Forest Ridge alive.

grantedPowers.name: Life Shaping
grantedPowers.description: Create simple life-shaped items from raw organic material

grantedPowers.name: Accelerated Growth
grantedPowers.description: Cause rapid plant growth in 100-ft. radius

name: The Jade Marquess
description: A jade-green crystalline parasite that bonds beneath the skin. Grants: +6 Constitution, fast healing 5, poison/disease immunity, photosynthesis (no food/water needed with sunlight). Body adjustment at will, metamorphosis 1/day. Slowly transforms host into plant creature over 1 year (irreversible). Removal deals 4d6 damage and -2 Constitution permanently.
darkSunNotes: Found in Blue Age halfling ruins. Some Forest Ridge druids willingly embrace the plant transformation.

grantedPowers.name: Body Adjustment
grantedPowers.description: Heal 1d12 damage, at will

grantedPowers.name: Metamorphosis
grantedPowers.description: Transform host into plant creature for 1 hour

grantedPowers.name: Photosynthesis
grantedPowers.description: No food or water needed while in sunlight

name: The Ma-kat Mannequin
description: A life-sized featureless humanoid of living tissue. Reshapes into a perfect duplicate of any seen humanoid, copying physical abilities. 200 hp, +10 natural armor. Follows complex commands for 24 hours, then reverts for 8 hours. Can carry 10 life-shaped items internally. Immune to mind-affecting effects.
darkSunNotes: Only three were created. One may still be active in the Forest Ridge, following orders from thousands of years ago.

grantedPowers.name: Shapeshift
grantedPowers.description: Reshape into a perfect duplicate of any seen humanoid

grantedPowers.name: Internal Storage
grantedPowers.description: Store up to 10 life-shaped items internally

name: The Arm of Radu
description: A life-shaped prosthetic arm that bonds with a lost limb. +8 Strength (that arm only), +3 natural armor. Extends up to 15 feet for reach. Fingers harden to claws (1d8) or flatten to blade (2d6 slashing). Regenerates 10 hp/round. Can detach and act independently for 10 rounds.
darkSunNotes: Created for halfling warrior Radu in the Blue Age. Currently believed held by a halfling chief in the Forest Ridge.

grantedPowers.name: Claw Attack
grantedPowers.description: Harden fingers to claws: 1d8 damage

grantedPowers.name: Blade Form
grantedPowers.description: Flatten to blade: 2d6 slashing damage

grantedPowers.name: Extended Reach
grantedPowers.description: Extend arm to 15 ft. for reach attacks

grantedPowers.name: Detach
grantedPowers.description: Detach arm to act independently for 10 rounds

name: Spirit Wombs
description: Large organic pods (6 ft. × 3 ft.). Suspended animation: no aging, no needs, immune to external effects. Emergence fully heals all damage, disease, poison, and ability damage. Two adjacent Wombs can swap consciousness between bodies (24 hours, permanent). Each Womb functions 100 times.
darkSunNotes: Found in buried rhulisti ruins, sometimes with Blue Age halflings still alive inside after thousands of years.

grantedPowers.name: Suspended Animation
grantedPowers.description: Enter stasis: no aging, no needs, immune to external effects

grantedPowers.name: Full Restoration
grantedPowers.description: Emerging fully heals all damage, disease, poison, and ability damage

grantedPowers.name: Consciousness Swap
grantedPowers.description: Two adjacent Wombs can permanently swap consciousness between bodies (24 hrs)

name: The Tongue of Glib the Mad
description: A slug-like organism replacing the user's tongue. Grants: all languages (permanent tongues), +10 to Bluff/Diplomacy/Intimidate, suggestion at will (DC 25), word of power stun 3/day, caustic spit (30 ft., 4d6 acid). 10% chance per social interaction of forcing dangerous truths (Will DC 20 to resist).
darkSunNotes: Created by Glib the Mad, an eccentric Blue Age life-shaper. Several copies may exist in ancient halfling ruins.

grantedPowers.name: Tongues
grantedPowers.description: Understand and speak all languages permanently

grantedPowers.name: Suggestion
grantedPowers.description: Suggestion at will (DC 25)

grantedPowers.name: Word of Power Stun
grantedPowers.description: Stun one creature, 3/day

grantedPowers.name: Caustic Spit
grantedPowers.description: 30 ft. range, 4d6 acid damage

name: Dorje of Energy Ray
description: A slender rod of deep crimson crystal, warm to the touch. Contains 50 charges of the Energy Ray power. The wielder points the dorje and a ray of destructive energy streaks toward the target.
darkSunNotes: Dorjes are the most common psionic items on Athas, crafted by the Order and sold through trusted merchants. This variety, tuned to fire energy, is popular among caravan guards crossing the Tablelands.

grantedPowers.name: Energy Ray
grantedPowers.description: Ranged touch attack, 1d6 energy damage. 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Crystal Shard
description: A translucent crystal rod that hums faintly when held. Contains 50 charges. When activated, it launches a shard of crystallized psychic energy at a target.
darkSunNotes: A staple of arena fighters and gladiators who lack natural psionic talent but need ranged options in the pit.

grantedPowers.name: Crystal Shard
grantedPowers.description: Ranged touch, 1d6 piercing damage. 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Inertial Armor
description: A pale blue crystal rod that generates a shimmering field of telekinetic force around the user. Contains 50 charges of Inertial Armor.
darkSunNotes: Highly prized by rogues and psionicists who eschew physical armor in the brutal Athasian heat. The invisible force field provides protection without the encumbrance.

grantedPowers.name: Inertial Armor
grantedPowers.description: +4 armor bonus to AC (force effect). 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Body Adjustment
description: A warm amber crystal rod pulsing with gentle light. Contains 50 charges of Body Adjustment, allowing the user to heal their own wounds through psychometabolic acceleration.
darkSunNotes: Worth more than water in the wastes. Elven traders and dune runners will kill for a charged dorje of healing.

grantedPowers.name: Body Adjustment
grantedPowers.description: Heal 1d12 damage to self. 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Psionic Charm
description: A rose-tinted crystal rod that resonates with the emotional center of the brain. Contains 50 charges of Psionic Charm.
darkSunNotes: Templars and noble merchants favor these dorjes for subtle manipulation. Banned in several city-states, though enforcement is predictably lax.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Charm
grantedPowers.description: Charm one humanoid (Will negates). 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Concussion Blast
description: A dense, smoke-gray crystal rod that vibrates with barely contained kinetic energy. Contains 50 charges of Concussion Blast.
darkSunNotes: A fearsome weapon favored by defilers-turned-psionicists and war-minded templars. The concussive force can scatter a charging gith raiding party.

grantedPowers.name: Concussion Blast
grantedPowers.description: Cone of concussive force, 2d6 damage. 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Ego Whip
description: A dark violet crystal rod streaked with silver. Contains 50 charges of Ego Whip, a telepathic assault that shreds the target's sense of self.
darkSunNotes: The Order considers ego whip dorjes distasteful but effective. Sorcerer-king interrogators use them freely.

grantedPowers.name: Ego Whip
grantedPowers.description: Deal 1d4 CHA damage and daze for 1 round (Will negates daze). 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Cloud Mind
description: An almost invisible crystal rod that seems to fade from perception. Contains 50 charges of Cloud Mind, rendering the user undetectable to a single target.
darkSunNotes: Essential equipment for Veiled Alliance agents and anyone trying to avoid a templar's gaze.

grantedPowers.name: Cloud Mind
grantedPowers.description: Become undetectable to one creature (Will negates). 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Telekinetic Force
description: A heavy crystal rod banded with iron. Contains 50 charges of Telekinetic Force, hurling objects or creatures with raw psychokinetic power.
darkSunNotes: Mul gladiators with psionic talent sometimes wield these in the arena for dramatic crowd-pleasing throws.

grantedPowers.name: Telekinetic Force
grantedPowers.description: Move object up to 250 lb or deal 1d6 damage. 50 charges.

name: Dorje of Thought Shield
description: A matte white crystal rod that seems to absorb psychic energy. Contains 50 charges of Thought Shield, granting +4 to saves vs. mind-affecting.
darkSunNotes: Common among merchants who trade in city-states where templars probe minds freely. A prudent investment.

grantedPowers.name: Thought Shield
grantedPowers.description: +4 to Will saves vs. mind-affecting effects. 50 charges.

name: Power Stone of Energy Ball
description: A fist-sized gemstone swirling with trapped energy. Single use. When activated, releases a burst of destructive energy in a 20-ft. radius.
darkSunNotes: Expensive but devastating. Caravan masters keep one or two for emergencies — a pack of belgoi or a thri-kreen raiding party can be scattered by a single well-placed detonation.

grantedPowers.name: Energy Ball
grantedPowers.description: 7d6 energy damage in 20-ft. radius. Single use, consumed on activation.

name: Power Stone of Teleport
description: A smooth crystal disc etched with spatial coordinates. Single use. Shatters upon activation, teleporting the user to a known location.
darkSunNotes: The ultimate escape plan. Wealthy nobles keep these hidden on their person at all times. The coordinates are usually keyed to a safe room in their estate.

grantedPowers.name: Teleport
grantedPowers.description: Teleport to known location. Single use, consumed on activation.

name: Power Stone of Body Purification
description: A small topaz sphere that glows with inner warmth. Single use. Purges poison and disease from the user's body.
darkSunNotes: Poison is everywhere on Athas — in the food, the water, the very air near some ruins. These stones are life-savers in the truest sense.

grantedPowers.name: Body Purification
grantedPowers.description: Restore 2 points of ability damage. Single use.

name: Power Stone of Psionic Blast
description: A jagged amethyst fragment crackling with telepathic static. Single use. Releases a devastating cone of psychic energy that stuns all creatures in its path.
darkSunNotes: Arena masters sometimes slip these to favored gladiators before matches against psionic opponents.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Blast
grantedPowers.description: Cone of psychic energy, stuns creatures for 1d4 rounds (Will negates). Single use.

name: Power Stone of Metamorphosis
description: A shifting, iridescent crystal that never looks the same twice. Single use. Allows the user to assume the form of any creature of similar size.
darkSunNotes: Veiled Alliance infiltrators use these to bypass templar checkpoints. Druids sometimes use them to take animal forms for scouting.

grantedPowers.name: Metamorphosis
grantedPowers.description: Assume the form of any creature of similar size for 1 min/level. Single use.

name: Power Stone of Dimension Door
description: A flat crystal wafer that shows a ghostly image of a distant location. Single use. Instantly transports the user up to 400 feet.
darkSunNotes: Short-range escape stones. Popular among thieves in the Elven Market and anyone who might need to leave a sorcerer-king's throne room in a hurry.

grantedPowers.name: Dimension Door
grantedPowers.description: Teleport up to 400 ft. Single use.

name: Power Stone of Dominate
description: A deep purple crystal that seems to watch you. Single use. Imposes total mental control over one creature.
darkSunNotes: Strictly controlled by the sorcerer-kings. Possession without templar authorization is punishable by slavery or death in most city-states.

grantedPowers.name: Dominate
grantedPowers.description: Dominate one creature (Will negates). Single use.

name: Power Stone of Death Urge
description: A black crystal that radiates despair. Single use. Compels a target to seek its own destruction.
darkSunNotes: An assassin's tool. The victim simply walks off a balcony or into the desert. No evidence, no struggle.

grantedPowers.name: Death Urge
grantedPowers.description: Target attempts to harm itself (Will negates). Single use.

name: Psicrown of the Telepath
description: A circlet of interlocking crystal plates that fits snugly on the brow. Contains 50 charges shared among multiple telepathic powers. A staple of mind-warriors and inquisitors.
darkSunNotes: The templar-wives of Nibenay wear these openly as symbols of authority. The Order crafts them for its most trusted operatives.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Charm
grantedPowers.description: Charm one humanoid (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Mindlink
grantedPowers.description: Telepathic link with willing creature (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Blast
grantedPowers.description: Cone of psychic energy, stuns (2 charges)

grantedPowers.name: Dominate
grantedPowers.description: Dominate one creature (2 charges)

name: Psicrown of the Nomad
description: A slim crystal headband etched with swirling portal glyphs. Contains 50 charges shared among psychoportation powers. Allows rapid travel across vast distances.
darkSunNotes: Priceless to merchant houses whose caravans must traverse the deadly wastes between city-states. A single psicrown can save weeks of desert travel.

grantedPowers.name: Burst
grantedPowers.description: +10 ft. movement for 1 round (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Dimension Door
grantedPowers.description: Teleport up to 400 ft. (2 charges)

grantedPowers.name: Teleport
grantedPowers.description: Teleport to known location (3 charges)

name: Psicrown of the Dominator
description: A crown of dark crystal spires that hums with oppressive psychic energy. Contains 50 charges of domination and control powers. An instrument of tyrants.
darkSunNotes: Crafted in the image of the sorcerer-kings' own psionic crowns. Possession alone is enough to earn a death sentence in most city-states — only the kings may dominate.

grantedPowers.name: Psionic Charm
grantedPowers.description: Charm one humanoid (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Dominate
grantedPowers.description: Total mental control over one creature (2 charges)

grantedPowers.name: Mass Domination
grantedPowers.description: Dominate multiple creatures (5 charges)

name: Psicrown of the Kineticist
description: A circlet of jagged crystal fragments suspended by invisible force. Contains 50 charges of psychokinetic destruction powers.
darkSunNotes: War-crowns. The armies of Hamanu and Nibenay equip their elite psionicist-captains with these to rain destruction upon enemies from behind the battle lines.

grantedPowers.name: Energy Ray
grantedPowers.description: 1d6 energy damage ray (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Concussion Blast
grantedPowers.description: Cone of force, 2d6 damage (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Energy Ball
grantedPowers.description: 7d6 energy damage in 20-ft. radius (2 charges)

grantedPowers.name: Telekinetic Force
grantedPowers.description: Move/hurl objects up to 250 lb (2 charges)

name: Psicrown of the Seer
description: A delicate crystal tiara with a single large eye-shaped gem at its center. Contains 50 charges of clairsentient perception powers.
darkSunNotes: Used by the Order's watchers to monitor threats across the Tablelands. Some say the Dragon himself used a greater version to observe all of Athas simultaneously.

grantedPowers.name: Clairvoyant Sense
grantedPowers.description: See/hear a distant location (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Ubiquitous Vision
grantedPowers.description: 360-degree vision, can't be flanked (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Remote Viewing
grantedPowers.description: Observe any known creature (2 charges)

grantedPowers.name: True Sight
grantedPowers.description: See through all illusions and invisibility (3 charges)

name: Psicrown of the Psychic Warrior
description: A battle-scarred crystal helm reinforced with chitin plates. Contains 50 charges of combat-enhancing psychometabolic powers.
darkSunNotes: Gladiatorial champions who survive long enough to earn their freedom often receive one of these as a prize from their patron noble. A crown for the arena-born king.

grantedPowers.name: Inertial Armor
grantedPowers.description: +4 armor bonus to AC (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Body Adjustment
grantedPowers.description: Heal 1d12 damage (1 charge)

grantedPowers.name: Painful Strike
grantedPowers.description: Next attack deals +2d6 damage and sickens (2 charges)

name: Thanak
breakageNote: On a natural 1 attack roll, make a DC 12 Reflex save or the weapon breaks.
description: A heavy, broad-bladed polearm unique to the pterran race. The thanak features a curved obsidian or bone blade mounted on a sturdy wooden haft, designed for powerful chopping strikes.
darkSunNotes: The signature weapon of pterran warriors. Outsiders rarely train with the thanak, though some gladiators have adopted it for its brutal effectiveness in the arena.

name: The Oglast Spade
description: This appears to be a strangely shaped shovel, the edges of its metal blade still razor sharp. The Oglast Spade functions as a metal halberd +1 and also confers a +5 competence bonus to all checks related to digging (Strength checks to dig a trench or clear a cave-in, Profession: Undertaker checks, etc). Faint evocation; CL 3rd; Craft Wondrous Item, Craft Magic Arms and Armor, soften earth and stone.
darkSunNotes: A family heirloom of House Oglast, descendants of undertakers who served in the Champion Wyan of Bodach's army. This unique weapon can be found in the ruins of Small Home in the Dead Lands.

name: Silk Wyrm Silk Outfit
description: Elegant clothing woven from the silk of silk wyrms by skilled artisans. The diaphanous material is surprisingly tough and grants a +1 armor bonus and fire resistance 2 to the wearer.
darkSunNotes: Silk wyrm silk is harvested from colonies along the Winding Way near the Dead Lands. The raw silk is extremely valuable, and finished garments are prized by nobles across the Tablelands.

name: Potion-Orb of Bull's Strength
description: A small obsidian orb that shatters when thrown against the body, releasing its contents. This potion-orb contains bull's strength, granting the user a +4 enhancement bonus to Strength for 3 minutes. CL 3rd.
darkSunNotes: Potion-orbs are the Athasian equivalent of potions, stored in small spheres of obsidian or crystal rather than glass vials. Used by pterran raiders and gladiators alike.

name: Potion-Orb of Cat's Grace
description: A small obsidian orb that shatters when thrown against the body, releasing its contents. This potion-orb contains cat's grace, granting the user a +4 enhancement bonus to Dexterity for 3 minutes. CL 3rd.
darkSunNotes: Potion-orbs are the Athasian equivalent of potions, stored in small spheres of obsidian or crystal rather than glass vials. Favored by rangers and rogues for pre-combat preparation.

name: Obsidian Claw Blood
description: A dark, viscous poison harvested and prepared by the Obsidian Claws pterran raiders. Functions as medium spider venom: injury, Fortitude DC 14, initial damage 1d4 Str, secondary damage 1d4 Str. Applied to weapons as a standard action.
darkSunNotes: Named for the exiled pterran tribe that perfected its extraction, this poison is derived from desert creatures near the Dead Lands. The Obsidian Claws coat their arrows and thanaks with it before ambushes.

name: Funerary Robes of Oglast
description: Ceremonial death shrouds passed down through generations of the Oglast merchant house. When worn within the woods of Small Home in the Dead Lands, these robes identify the wearer as a pilgrim fulfilling the family's ancient debt, granting safe passage through the hostile undead territory. The robes must be donned before entering the woods, or the undead spirits will not recognize the wearer as a pilgrim.
darkSunNotes: Once a generation, four members of the Oglast family must wear these robes and make a pilgrimage to Small Home to perform a ceremony of appeasement. Failure to do so causes a wasting curse to afflict four random family members.
